verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 is a half-type ABC transporter with canonical lysosomal localization. Multiple independent studies using fluorescence microscopy and subcellular fractionation document exclusive ER and lysosomal residence. The N-terminal TMD0 domain contains a required localization signal that actively sequesters the protein away from the plasma membrane. No cell-surface-directed therapeutics, no documented cell-state surface translocation, no narrow-lineage surface restriction, no lysosomal-exocytosis evidence, and no documented PM cycling or stable TM-partner anchoring. Full-length ABCB9 is not accessible from the extracellular face under its baseline biology.
